source.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

claim_chain: The synergistic binding of ATP with pseudosubstrate inhibitor IP20 revealed a novel nucleotide binding site distinct from the Rossmann fold
BLU0588 has broken PKA-C's finely tuned machinery by defining two subsites in the phosphate-organizing site through hydrophobic interactions
BLU0588 employs shape complementation to fill the entire active site cleft with hydrophobic elements
BLU0588 binding translates to striking biochemical readouts including picomolar Ki and increased thermal stability
The unique complementarity of the four rings of BLU0588 accounts for its high potency and unique binding properties
These structural features provide a framework for designing kinase inhibitors with improved cellular efficacy

typical_pattern: Historical context establishing the novelty of the binding mechanism
Detailed structural description of how BLU0588 occupies the active site
Biochemical and biophysical characterization of binding properties
Comparison with other inhibitors to highlight unique features
Implications for inhibitor design and future research directions

likely_priorities: Rigorous kinetic comparison with other PKA-C inhibitors
Direct experimental validation of effects on PKA-C dynamics and substrate binding synergy
Mechanistic explanation of selectivity for BLU0588 binding to PKA
Confirmation of cellular effects previously attributed to H89
likely_attacks: Lack of complete kinetic experiments comparing BLU0588 to other inhibitors
Absence of NMR or MD simulation data on dynamics
Need for cellular validation studies to replace H89 experiments
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The paper explicitly states 'Our goal here is not to explain the selectivity for BLU0588 binding to PKA nor to design more selective inhibitors' and 'It will now be essential to carry out additional kinetic experiments to rigorously compare BLU0588 to other PKA-C inhibitors. Experiments to define and compare the effect on PKA-C dynamics and substrate binding synergy with other inhibitors using techniques such as NMR, MD simulations, SPR and crystallography are already under way.' The authors also note 'Others now need to revisit the cellular H89 studies to confirm which effects are unambiguous due to inhibition of PKA.'

examples.quoted_phrase: BLU0588 has not only broken PKA-C's finely tuned machinery
We propose that the unique complementarity of the four rings of BLU0588 to the conserved features of the active site cleft accounts for its high potency
BLU0588 binding to PKA-C translates to striking biochemical readouts
This could suggest that BLU0588 must first tether into the active site of PKA-C
Our goal here is not to explain the selectivity for BLU0588 binding to PKA

phrase_evidence.phrase: The synergistic binding of ATP with the pseudosubstrate inhibitor IP20 and two Mg2+ ions, first captured in a crystal structure in 1993, revealed what was at the time a novel nucleotide binding site
BLU0588 has not only broken PKA-C's finely tuned machinery that positions the nucleotide
the indane and pyrrolidine rings of BLU0588 have clearly also defined the two subsites in the PO-site
Instead of the electrophilic phosphate and magnesium-mediated interactions in the PO-site which are essential for catalysis
the BLU0588-bound conformation is stabilized primarily by hydrophobic interactions
BLU0588 employs a highly effective molecular strategy with shape complementation
BLU0588 binding to PKA-C translates to striking biochemical readouts
We observed an increase in thermal stability in the presence of BLU0588
This could suggest that BLU0588 must first tether into the active site of PKA-C rather than slipping in like ATP or H89
longer residence times of drugs with their targets, as a result of slower off-rates, is a useful mechanism to extend in-vivo efficacy
We predict that BLU0588 also hinders binding of the pseudosubstrate inhibitor RIα
Our analysis indicates that presence of BLU0588 results not only in potent inhibition of PKA-C phosphorylation
This contrasts with typical ATP-competitive kinase inhibitors that ineffectively exploit most of the active site pockets
Our goal here is not to explain the selectivity for BLU0588 binding to PKA nor to design more selective inhibitors
Others now need to revisit the cellular H89 studies to confirm which effects are unambiguous due to inhibition of PKA
We propose that the unique complementarity of the four rings of BLU0588 to the conserved features of the active site cleft accounts for its high potency
It will now be essential to carry out additional kinetic experiments to rigorously compare BLU0588 to other PKA-C inhibitors
Taken together, the structural features of BLU0588 elucidated here provide a framework for designing kinase inhibitors with improved cellular efficacy
The high-resolution crystal structure not only captures the inhibitor's unusual T-shaped geometry
shows how the four rings of BLU0588 serve as surrogates for ATP's adenosine and phosphate-organizing sites
The structure also shows how BLU0588 nucleates an open but stable conformation
BLU0588 also abolishes the synergistic high-affinity binding of the physiological pseudosubstrate inhibitor
The residence time of BLU0588, measured by surface plasmon residence, contributes to its picomolar affinity
These molecular insights provide a valuable framework for dissecting the organization of the active site cleft
Comparison of small molecule PKA-C inhibitors
B-factors indicate changes of PKA-C:BLU0588 dynamics
BLU0588 nucleates an extensive hydrophobic network of interactions

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. No external references, author names, or invented numbers were included. The profile accurately reflects the paper's assertive tone, mechanistic focus, and structural biology emphasis while respecting the stated limitations regarding selectivity mechanisms and need for additional kinetic experiments.

shows how the four rings of BLU0588 serve as surrogates for ATP's adenosine and phosphate-organizing sites
BLU0588 has not only broken PKA-C's finely tuned machinery
